_comment: DEMO stand-in export of user feedback (survey verbatims + NPS follow-ups) for June 2026. Verbatims are illustrative.
source: In-app NPS survey + post-session feedback prompts

summary.note: Sentiment is strongly positive overall; the product is well-liked. Criticism is concentrated and specific, not broad dissatisfaction.

positiveVerbatims.text: AI Assist cut my prep time in half — I close more deals in a day than I used to in three.
The pipeline suggestions are scary accurate. My whole team switched over in a week.
Best CRM update in years. Onboarding new reps is finally painless.
Love opening the app in the morning and seeing exactly which deals are heating up.
Forecasting is finally trustworthy — when the data is in, it's gold.

criticalVerbatims.text: I check my deals on my phone between meetings all day — but there's no way I'm typing a call summary into all those fields while I'm walking into the next one. I do it Friday, if I remember.
I'm on back-to-back calls. I can glance at the deal while I'm dialing, but stopping to log every call and update the stage? No time. It piles up.
The app is great for seeing what's next. Updating it is the problem — it feels like paperwork I do after hours, not something I can do live.
I'm in my car or at client sites most of the day. I open HeroForce to see my pipeline, but I can't safely update a deal from the road. Give me a voice note and be done.
I look at my deals every single morning. But logging activity is 8 clicks and 5 fields — I batch it Sunday night or just don't.
I sell, I don't do data entry. If AI Assist could just write the update from my call and let me tap 'yes', I'd actually keep the CRM current.
Nothing wrong with finding my deals — they're right there when I log in. It's that updating them mid-day is a hassle when you're never at a desk.
